full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding the step-by-step algorithm of DCD from the paper
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Read the paper 'A Dual Coordinate Descent Method for Large-scale Linear SVM' and explain the algorithm as a step-by-step sequence from input to output, with references to equations.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided a structured outline of the algorithm steps; I cross-checked each step with the paper equations (Eq. 4, 6-12) and refined the description to be specific to this paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Not applicable — no code in Task 1.1.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Every algorithm step was verified against the paper: random permutation (Section 3.1), closure-form update (Eq. 9-11), w update (Eq. 12), stopping condition (Eq. 17).

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Drafting the Final Summary Sentence for Task 1.1
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Write a one-sentence summary of the DCD paper that covers: what problem it solves, and what the authors claim makes it better than existing alternatives.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided a template sentence which I edited to accurately reflect the O(log(1/eps)) convergence claim and the specific baselines mentioned in the paper (Pegasos, TRON, SVMperf).
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Not applicable — no code in Task 1.1.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified that all claims in the summary sentence are supported by the abstract and Section 1 of the paper.
